title: dalbergia gilbertii
intro: Dalbergia gilbertii, also known as the Philippine rosewood or narra tree, is a species of flowering plant in the family Fabaceae. It is a valuable timber tree that has been used for centuries in traditional medicine, valued for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and antiviral properties. The bark, leaves, and roots of the Dalbergia gilbertii plant are used to make herbal preparations that offer various health benefits, such as reducing fever, relieving pain, and treating respiratory issues. Herbal preparations of Dalbergia gilbertii include decoctions, infusions, and ointments, which are often used to treat a range of health conditions in traditional medicine.
